title: Mammalian Cell Staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4qj16","text":"This protocol details how to stain mammalian cells cultured on a 96-well plate. Actin filaments, focal adhesion sites (as indicated by the presence of vinculin), and nuclei will be stained.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ku2l","text":"4% Paraformaldehyde solution","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"98ku8","text":"0.1% Triton X-100 solution in PBS","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a7ftr","text":"Blocking buffer (PBS + 1% bovine serum albumin)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2a3e4","text":"Washing buffer (PBS + 0.05% Tween-20)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1d1n2","text":"Anti-vinculin solution (1:500 in blocking buffer)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7aqf1","text":"TRITC-conjugated phalloidin and FITC-conjugated antivinculin secondary antibody solution (1:1:248, TRITC:FITC:blocking buffer) referred to as FITC:TRITC","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fddhj","text":"DAPI solution (1:249 DAPI:blocking buffer)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"des2o","text":"Phosphate buffered saline (PBS)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 100 µL FITC:TRITC solution, cover in foil.
Add 250 µL of Anti-Vinculin and blocking buffer mixture.
Add 100 µL of 4 % volume paraformaldehyde solution.
Label the flask with: Cell line Passage number Date Your intials
Add 100 µL of 0.1 % volume Triton X-100.
Wash twice with 100 µL washing buffer. Washing buffer is PBS with the detergent Tween-20.
Add 100 µL blocking buffer. Blocking buffer is PBS with BSA (bovine serum albumin) and is used to prevent unspecific binding.
Wash twice with 100 µL washing buffer.
Add 100 µL of DAPI solution, cover in foil.
Image your cells using UV, Blue, and Green excitation.
creator: Kenneth Schackart
